content:
Hello @Chri.z @bunthius 

Today is the economic forum, and I hope you can talk a bit about the UE5 side of things. I don’t want to push you for leaks, but I also can’t quite understand why the economic cycle in UE5 hasn’t started yet. UE5 already looks amazing, and it’s clear that everyone is eager to spend time there.

You’ve previously mentioned topics like anti-cheat, but aside from that, what are the other obstacles preventing it from starting? SURGE is a fantastic module—absolutely yes—but what can we expect this year in terms of open-world missions/economy where we can actually use our ships?

With the rental system and SAGE being solid and evolving, today seems like a great day to talk a lot about UE5.

Thanks!

author: 《VΛ》 KATIREcon Vantarah
content: honestly it's the anti-cheat. a while ago we made a simple game mode to go through the process of all these portions of the economic cycle in UE5 as you put it. Game mode was called Flower Hunt. It was the simplest form of open world, multiplayer mechanics to find the flower randomly spawned and then give the winner some on chain units of the mni flower. We got everything working up until the onchain rewards. Which resulted in pushback from the part of the team that would be implementing that last mile due to security concerns. We could have been able to use that same process for everything else. But alas, we continue to build the necessary mechanics up until that last mile that keeps getting kicked down the roadmap in favor of more secure direct on chain mechanics like in SAGE Labs, eventually we'll have the ability to put full focus on it. But to their validation, it only takes less than 24 hours to edit the client to cheat the random placement and navigation mechanism to find the rewards client side, so it all has to be validated with an authorative server before onchain rewards can be distributed. So, they're not wrong just have other priorities before tackling this one fully head on.

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: Team is grinding hard in every department. I tend to not be able to engage unless pinged directly. I do follow along a lot but there's always lots to do. When progression kicks in there will be something for everyone. The main thing with the pre-C4 XP carrying over is that players who racked up those points will be able to pull them into the new Council Rank perk system. But once we turn on proper progression we must shed pay to win mechanics. We do that by leveling the playing field so no initial investment gives you an edge on the initial unlocking of perks. So, for example, the ability to fly a certain class ship in fleet command/c4 will need to be earned. However, once you earn it, grinding for the ability to actually purchase or craft that class of ship doesn't increase you actual skill anymore just shows you have a strong tolerance for tedium. This is where purchasing or the investment player can reduce that grinding. IMO as long as the skill unlock was equally committed then it's a level playing field and having those "investor" style players is good for everyone because it brings value into the overall economy which makes grinding for it worth it to those who can't. Also, just because players can't bring their investments into the assets doesn't mean they're fully disadvantaged, theoretically they have more time to dedicate to the game and are advantaged for the engagement player, as you mentioned. So, having avenues that work for both types of players and also evening the unlocks to gametime played, it creates the best balance imo.  With cnfts it was never part of council rank leveling to allow the rarity to gain xp at a faster rate. The faster rate xp gain was for ability perks for that character, which indirectly boosts ship performance which then indirectly boosts xp events that accrue towards council rank and then go towards CR perks like ship fleet size, plot ownership etc. That system is still a bit off until cnft leveling tech is prioritized again. With unreal there are many different tracks to keep people engaged and many of those xp tracks will have crossover into C4/FC.   One complete example of this is Ship Mastery. You have to own the ship to accrue that ship's xp towards that ship's Ship Mastery track when you use it. That track rewards the player with items related to that ship's performance like components of an increased tier to help boost the performance of your ship and a range of cosmetics. You'll also get the unreal only resource that goes towards loot drops and craft recipes. You'll also get Pilot XP on your account which goes towards SAGE Pilot XP and Council Rank Renown. SAGE Pilot XP unlocks ship class tiers, Renown can be spent on fleet sizes and plot ownership counts and many other things. All just from owning a ship and using it in races, dogfighting, general combat in unreal, booking flight hours and peforming skillful maneuvers.

author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content:
Yeah no problem.

I've written & published two full length fiction novels, and a couple years ago wrote a screenplay for season 2 of a popular PlanetSide 2 machinima.

After hearing ZeSKK speak about the Sogmians during Lore Keepers 1 (thank you @𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul), I keep having this Sogmian scene playing in my head that I want to script out and get animated pending lore consultation with ZeSKK 

Possible that I might combine this with Raspy's short film blitz, but if it's not a good fit then I'll PIP it separate.

The animator used UE5 and models from the PS2 game with presets and automation to churn out a really, really great product.

He described his process here in one of my other podcast series
https://youtu.be/BZczhnF2BX4?si=Eo7HL_Gphu1abXKE&t=6212

And described it via text here:

in theory, UE has all the capabilities
but I prefer to animate in blender and I think it's a bit easier to get what you want & then export it as fbx into unreal (there are also export plugins to make it easier)

In unreal you will then create the set, then put the models & characters in there & create a sequence
In that sequence you can give the character the imported animations, create the cameras, animate the cameras, time the camera shots etc.
also you dont want to hand animate everything anyways - I used animation presets from mixamo (which had to adjust in blender) & used AI motion tracking in wonder dynamics (which I had to heavily clean up in blender)

Combined all of this with some hand animated parts & some "movie magic" to hide some flaws & voila

author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content:
So if I were to have access to a Sogmian model and SA environmental models...I could write the Sogmian script, partner with an SA community animator who can use UE5, and then produce that 10 minute short sequence.

It would look really, really dope (in my opinion)

author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content: We haven't been able to share the working game models because of some specific project settings to make the characters work. The pipeline is still evolving as new species are added in. So, migrating assets to another project isn't very clean cut and would require some handholding or documentation that isn't ready for public consumption at the moment. Also, specifically for the sogmian, they haven't been treated to proper production models as they're meant to come later. Currently they're in a loaner/clay state and wouldnt recommend working with them until they get the proper production pass to become game ready. Which means no usable UVs or textures, so there would be a lot of extra work to make them usable even in a pre-rendering environment.

I'm not trying to poke holes in the system, I'm trying to understand the rationale behind it. Thank you.

author: ROME | Winston [ROME]
content:
Hello everyone,
The notion that GAO ships would not be burned even in the HRZ has been discussed in the past — but I have been thinking about it, and the system could go even further.

Proposal:
Crafted Ships: No respawn upon destruction — even in PvE encounters.,Similar to EVE Online, if you lose a crafted ship, it’s gone. Cargo is dropped with a partial burn. This helps regulate the amount of crafted ships in circulation, coupled with limited crafting quantities. 
GAO Ships: Remain respawnable, with a timer getting progressively longer, providing players with a long-term asset advantage, a "lifetime" investment for those planning to commit to Star Atlas for years.,

Zone Mechanics:

  Safe Zones:Minimal to no combat.,Both crafted and GAO Ships respawn with components after destruction.,Cargo drops without burn, it still has to be recovered but remains locked to its owner for a considerably longer period of time.,Rewards are minimal — enough to cover consumables, but no real profit.,Serves as a training and testing ground for new players.,
  Medium-Risk Zone:Crafted ships are permanently burned on death.,Crew survive using escape pods and automatically respawn (until Search & Rescue gameplay is introduced, where players or NPCs can recover them for a fee).,Cargo and components are dropped into space. Medium timer to lock the dropped cargo to its owner.,Random % of cargo is burned during destruction.,
  High-Risk Zone:Crafted ships are permanently burned on death.,No lock timer for cargo and component drops — first come, first served looting.,Crew casualties: while crew generally respawn, a percentage could be permanently KIA.,Medical ships and officers can reduce KIA rates, adding another layer of strategic fleet composition and logistics.,
 Extreme Scenarios (Death during High-end Raids, Flying into Black Holes, Suns, etc.):Full burn: ship, components, cargo, and crew permanently lost. This would also apply to GAO ships, only the hull would respawn with t0 components.,The ultimate risk for ultimate rewards.,

Benefits of This System:
Creates an additional resource sink with crafted ships having a high turnover.,Encourages careful planning, salvage opportunities and future Search & Rescue missions.,Preserves GAO ships’ value for long-term players without stifling crafting markets.,Makes losses meaningful and victories sweeter.,

GAO Ships: Remain respawnable, with a timer getting progressively longer, providing players with a long-term asset advantage, a "lifetime" investment for those planning to commit to Star Atlas for years. , Zone Mechanics: Safe Zones: Minimal to no combat. , Both crafted and GAO Ships respawn with components after destruction. , Cargo drops without burn, it still has to be recovered but remains locked to its owner for a considerably longer period of time. , Rewards are minimal — enough to cover consumables, but no real profit. , Serves as a training and testing ground for new players. , Medium-Risk Zone: Crafted ships are permanently burned on death. , Crew survive using escape pods and automatically respawn (until Search & Rescue gameplay is introduced, where players or NPCs can recover them for a fee). , Cargo and components are dropped into space. Medium timer to lock the dropped cargo to its owner. , Random % of cargo is burned during destruction. , High-Risk Zone: Crafted ships are permanently burned on death. , No lock timer for cargo and component drops — first come, first served looting. , Crew casualties: while crew generally respawn, a percentage could be permanently KIA. , Medical ships and officers can reduce KIA rates, adding another layer of strategic fleet composition and logistics. , Extreme Scenarios (Death during High-end Raids, Flying into Black Holes, Suns, etc.): Full burn: ship, components, cargo, and crew permanently lost. This would also apply to GAO ships, only the hull would respawn with t0 components. , The ultimate risk for ultimate rewards. , Benefits of This System: Creates an additional resource sink with crafted ships having a high turnover. , Encourages careful planning, salvage opportunities and future Search & Rescue missions. , Preserves GAO ships’ value for long-term players without stifling crafting markets. , Makes losses meaningful and victories sweeter. ,

author: ROME | Winston [ROME]
content: I will just add, I think a lot of people were against this concept because of fear of overinflation of crafted ships diminishing the value of GAO ships. With crafted ships being essentially consumables and getting burnt even with PvE deaths, hopefully this bring a more balanced system that brings value to the GAO ships while providing a deep sink of resources with the crafting of new consumable ships. Stimulating the economy by bringing more demand for Fuel/Food/Tools/Ammo and rare resources potentially from UE5.
